data.meta_description: Plan a grandparents yacht day Marbella with our fleet of family-friendly boats. Licensed skipper, soft drinks & tapas included. Book your 8-hour charter from €2,299.
data.summary: A stress-free grandparents yacht day Marbella from Puerto Banús – 8-hour charters on spacious flybridges or motor yachts with a licensed skipper, soft drinks, beer, wine, cava, light snacks, and IVA included. Choose from 12.5m–29m boats that accommodate 9–12 guests comfortably.
key_facts: 8-hour (full-day) charters from €2,299 on Astondoa 40 or Azimut 39 (12.5m, 9–11 pax).
All charters include licensed skipper, fuel, insurance, VAT (21%), water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava & light snacks.
Larger options: Mangusta 80 (24m, 12 pax, jet ski included) from €4,719 for 4h.
Departures from Puerto Banús – no long transfers, easy boarding for all ages.
Fleet includes stable flybridge yachts (Azimut 58, Sunseeker Predator 58) with shaded saloons & sun pads.
Half-day (4h) also available – ideal for a relaxed afternoon with grandchildren.
All boats have onboard WC, safety equipment, and first-aid kit.
Customise your route: Marbella coastline, Cabopino, or a quiet anchorage at Cala del Faro.

faq.q: What is the best boat for a grandparents yacht day Marbella?
faq.a: For a group of up to 9 guests we recommend the Astondoa 40 (12.5m) at €2,299 for 8 hours – it has a large aft deck, saloon seating, and easy access. For up to 12 guests, the Azimut 58 Flybridge (17.6m) offers a shaded flybridge, indoor saloon, and stable ride. All include a licensed skipper, soft drinks, beer, wine, cava, light snacks, and VAT.

faq.q: Are the boats accessible for elderly guests?
faq.a: Yes. All our boats depart from Puerto Banús pontoons with level gangways. The Astondoa 40 and Azimut 39 have low freeboard and wide side decks. Flybridge models require climbing a ladder to the upper deck, but the main deck has full seating and a WC. We can advise on the best option for your group.

faq.q: What is included in the price?
faq.a: Every charter includes a licensed skipper, fuel, insurance, Spanish IVA (21%), water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, and light snacks. No hidden costs. For the Mangusta 80 (4h minimum, from €4,719) a jet ski is also included. Prices for other boats are available on request via WhatsApp.

faq.q: Can we bring our own food on board?
faq.a: Absolutely. You are welcome to bring your own food and cake. We provide soft drinks, beer, wine, cava, and light snacks. Many families bring a birthday cake or a picnic. Just let us know in advance if you need a cooler or extra storage.

faq.q: What is the best itinerary for a multi-generational group?
faq.a: A popular route leaves Puerto Banús east towards Cabopino, passing the Golden Mile and Marbella Marina. You can anchor at Cala del Faro for swimming in calm waters. Alternatively, head west to Estepona for a quieter coastline. The skipper will adjust the speed and stops to suit all ages.

faq.q: How many people can we bring?
faq.a: Our largest boats (Mangusta 80, Canados 86, Ferretti 94, Maiora 26m) carry up to 12 guests. The Azimut 39 holds 11, the Astondoa 40 holds 9. For a group of 5 or fewer, the Dubhe (8m, 5 pax) is a cosy option. All capacities include the skipper.

Your safety is our priority. The skipper will assess conditions on the day. If the weather is unsafe, we can reschedule your charter at no extra cost or offer a full refund. Marbella enjoys 300+ sunny days a year, so cancellations are rare.

faq.q: How do I book a grandparents yacht day Marbella?
faq.a: You can book directly on our website or contact us via WhatsApp. For the Astondoa 40 or Azimut 39, select the 8-hour option at €2,299. For other boats, send us a message with your preferred date, group size, and any special requests. We will confirm availability within 1 hour.

data.body_html:
Planning a grandparents yacht day Marbella means finding a boat that is comfortable, safe, and easy to board for all ages. Our fleet from Puerto Banús includes spacious motor yachts and flybridge cruisers with shaded saloons, onboard WC, and a licensed skipper who handles navigation, anchoring, and safety. Every charter includes soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, fuel, insurance, and Spanish IVA (21%) – so you can focus on relaxing with your grandchildren.

Why choose a full-day charter for a multi-generational group?

An 8-hour charter (€2,299 on the Astondoa 40 or Azimut 39) gives you time to cruise the Marbella coastline, stop for a swim, enjoy lunch on board, and return without rushing. Half-day (4-hour) options are also available for shorter attention spans. The slower pace suits grandparents who want to sunbathe on the aft deck while younger family members use the inflatable tender or snorkel gear. All boats depart from Puerto Banús, so there are no long transfers.

Our recommended boats for grandparents and grandchildren

BoatLengthMax guestsStarting price (8h)Key feature
Astondoa 4012.5 m9€2,299Easy boarding, large aft deck
Azimut 3912.5 m11€2,299Spacious saloon, WC
Azimut 58 Flybridge17.6 m12on requestShaded flybridge, sun pads
Mangusta 8024 m12€4,719 (4h min)Jet ski included, huge deck

For groups of 5 or fewer, the Dubhe (8m, 5 pax) is a cosy alternative. All prices include skipper, fuel, drinks, snacks, insurance, and VAT.

What to expect on the day

  • Meeting point: Puerto Banús, main dock. Our skipper will greet you at the boat.
  • Safety briefing: Life jackets, emergency procedures, and WC location – takes 5 minutes.
  • Cruising: Gentle speed (8–12 knots) along the coast. You can ask the skipper to stop at any time for photos or swimming.
  • Anchoring: Popular spots include Cala del Faro (calm, shallow water) or off Cabopino beach.
  • Lunch: Bring your own food or order a catering hamper (available on request). We provide drinks and snacks.
  • Return: Back at Puerto Banús by 19:00 (8h charter) or 15:00 (4h charter).

Accessibility and comfort

All our boats have a main deck with seating, a shaded saloon, and an onboard WC. The Astondoa 40 and Azimut 39 have low freeboard and wide side decks, making boarding easy. Flybridge models require climbing a ladder to the upper deck, but the main deck is fully accessible. If a family member uses a walking stick or has limited mobility, let us know in advance and we will recommend the most suitable boat. The licensed skipper will adjust the speed and route to ensure a smooth ride.

Customising your itinerary

Your skipper can tailor the route to your preferences. A typical full-day itinerary leaves Puerto Banús east towards Cabopino, passing Marbella Marina and the Golden Mile. After anchoring for a swim, you can continue to Cala Cortés or return via the Río Verde estuary. Alternatively, head west to Estepona or Sotogrande for a quieter coastline. The choice is yours – just tell the skipper what you enjoy.

What to bring

  • Sunscreen, hats, and sunglasses – the Costa del Sol sun is strong.
  • Swimwear and towels – towels are not provided on all boats.
  • Light jacket or jumper – sea breezes can be cool even in summer.
  • Food and cake – we supply drinks and snacks, but you can bring your own.
  • Camera – you will want photos of the coastline and the family.

For more ideas, explore our experiences page or contact us via WhatsApp for personalised advice.

Frequently asked questions

What is the best boat for a grandparents yacht day Marbella?

For a group of up to 9 guests we recommend the Astondoa 40 (12.5m) at €2,299 for 8 hours – it has a large aft deck, saloon seating, and easy access. For up to 12 guests, the Azimut 58 Flybridge (17.6m) offers a shaded flybridge, indoor saloon, and stable ride. All include a licensed skipper, soft drinks, beer, wine, cava, light snacks, and VAT.

Are the boats accessible for elderly guests?

Yes. All our boats depart from Puerto Banús pontoons with level gangways. The Astondoa 40 and Azimut 39 have low freeboard and wide side decks. Flybridge models require climbing a ladder to the upper deck, but the main deck has full seating and a WC. We can advise on the best option for your group.

What is included in the price?

Every charter includes a licensed skipper, fuel, insurance, Spanish IVA (21%), water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, and light snacks. No hidden costs. For the Mangusta 80 (4h minimum, from €4,719) a jet ski is also included. Prices for other boats are available on request via WhatsApp.

Can we bring our own food on board?

Absolutely. You are welcome to bring your own food and cake. We provide soft drinks, beer, wine, cava, and light snacks. Many families bring a birthday cake or a picnic. Just let us know in advance if you need a cooler or extra storage.

What is the best itinerary for a multi-generational group?

A popular route leaves Puerto Banús east towards Cabopino, passing the Golden Mile and Marbella Marina. You can anchor at Cala del Faro for swimming in calm waters. Alternatively, head west to Estepona for a quieter coastline. The skipper will adjust the speed and stops to suit all ages.

How many people can we bring?

Our largest boats (Mangusta 80, Canados 86, Ferretti 94, Maiora 26m) carry up to 12 guests. The Azimut 39 holds 11, the Astondoa 40 holds 9. For a group of 5 or fewer, the Dubhe (8m, 5 pax) is a cosy option. All capacities include the skipper.

What happens in bad weather?

Your safety is our priority. The skipper will assess conditions on the day. If the weather is unsafe, we can reschedule your charter at no extra cost or offer a full refund. Marbella enjoys 300+ sunny days a year, so cancellations are rare.

How do I book a grandparents yacht day Marbella?

You can book directly on our website or contact us via WhatsApp. For the Astondoa 40 or Azimut 39, select the 8-hour option at €2,299. For other boats, send us a message with your preferred date, group size, and any special requests. We will confirm availability within 1 hour.
